winners.name: Atitih Yati Robert

heritage.costume: Sinuangga — Penampang-style adaptation in black and gold, adorned with hand-beaded himpogot sash and manik breastplate, representing the highland Dusun tradition of Tamparuli

winners.bio: Atitih Yati Robert was crowned the 65th Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan 2025 at Hongkod Koisaan, Penampang, triumphing over 51 finalists on 31 May 2025. A 26-year-old Bachelor of Education (Malay Language) graduate from Universiti Putra Malaysia, her victory was especially poignant — it was her second attempt, having first competed representing Kota Marudu eight years earlier. Her prize included RM17,500 cash, a RM35,000 NBUC scholarship, and a trip to South Korea.

winners.name: Vanessa Gunsanad

heritage.costume: Souduh Dusun in deep indigo and silver, featuring hand-stitched linangkit panels and togkol headpiece

winners.bio: Vanessa's mastery of Dusun oral poetry (tontolian) and her intricate knowledge of highland Dusun weaving traditions set her apart in the 2024 competition, making her the first Tambunan-origin winner in a decade.

winners.name: Cynthia Rambilin

heritage.costume: Full Rungus bridal regalia — tinangkong (black wrap skirt) with intricate manik collar, arm rings, and brass coil anklets

winners.bio: Cynthia's victory in 2023 was historic — the first Rungus queen from Kudat to win the KDCA Sabah crown. Her breathtaking performance of the monogit dance and her encyclopedic knowledge of Rungus ritual songs left the audience spellbound.

winners.name: Roselind Mansul

heritage.costume: Warrior's regalia featuring kagang (ceremonial rattan cap), woven sinamot vest, and traditional parang (blade) sash

winners.bio: Roselind is a formidable advocate for Murut cultural heritage, selected as the 2025 MRK (Momogun Rungus Kadazan) representative for her deep knowledge of traditional Murut martial arts, craft, and oral history.

winners.name: Patricia Limundok

heritage.costume: Contemporary-traditional Sinuangga' reinterpreted with ikat-dyed panels and modern silhouette, representing the KDM diaspora identity

winners.bio: Born and raised in Kuala Lumpur but deeply rooted in her Kadazan Dusun heritage, Patricia won hearts at KDCA Klang Valley 2025 with her bilingual cultural presentation and her advocacy for urban indigenous identity.

winners.name: Sheryl Bunsin

heritage.costume: Pinamorokan Papar dress in red and black silk, featuring distinctive gold thread embroidery and the traditional suluk head tie

winners.bio: Sheryl moved to KL to pursue a career in medicine but never lost her connection to Papar. Her 2024 KDCA KL victory was powered by an emotionally charged cultural monologue delivered entirely in Kadazan, moved the audience to tears.

winners.name: Janelle Mumin

heritage.costume: Full ceremonial Rungus attire: black and red woven dress, layered manik (glass bead) necklaces, and brass decorative rings

winners.bio: A cultural warrior in the truest sense, Janelle has spent years documenting endangered Rungus chants and weaving patterns in Pitas. Her MRK win at KDCA KL 2025 is a testament to her lifelong commitment to Rungus preservation.

winners.name: Mellisa Gansai

heritage.costume: Highland Kadazan attire in forest green and gold, featuring the distinctive suyang headdress and hand-pounded silver accessories

winners.bio: Mellisa works as a civil servant in Putrajaya but dedicates her weekends to teaching Kadazan folk songs at the community centre. Her calm authority and cultural depth won the 2025 KDCA Putrajaya crown.

winners.name: Doreen Bujang

heritage.costume: Dusun Keningau ceremonial dress in deep maroon and ivory, with handwoven banat (cloth) panels and silver coin accessories

winners.bio: A graphic designer by profession, Doreen's creative mind extends to reviving traditional Dusun textile patterns for contemporary fashion. Her cultural vision and eloquent advocacy earned her the 2024 KDCA Putrajaya title.

winners.name: Lorraine Sibog

heritage.costume: Tagal warrior dress in deep ochre and black woven sinamot, featuring feathered ceremonial headpiece and traditional boar tusk ornaments

winners.bio: A researcher specialising in Murut customary law (adat), Lorraine uses her legal expertise to advocate for indigenous land rights. Her MRK win at KDCA Putrajaya 2025 recognised her as a pillar of Murut cultural resilience.

winners.name: Vivienne Dungkang

heritage.costume: Kota Marudu coastal Kadazan attire in sea-blue and gold, with distinctive perak (silver) headwear and mother-of-pearl accessories

winners.bio: Vivienne is a secondary school teacher in Shah Alam who channels her cultural knowledge into curriculum development. Her 2025 KDCA Selangor victory highlighted the importance of KDM education in diaspora communities.

winners.name: Charlene Asli

heritage.costume: Kadazan Tuaran dress in emerald and silver with the distinctive kagang headdress and layered beaded neck ornaments

winners.bio: An environmental scientist and passionate cultural keeper, Charlene bridges traditional ecological knowledge of the KDM people with modern conservation science. Her MRK win at KDCA Selangor 2025 celebrates this unique intersection.

winners.name: Irene Mansun

heritage.costume: Souduh Dusun highland weave in ivory and crimson, featuring handmade tupi' (shoulder wrap) and tinansad (front panel) with geometric motifs

winners.bio: An architect based in Johor Bahru, Irene incorporates traditional KDM longhouse architectural elements into her modern designs. Her 2025 KDCA JB win celebrated her vision of indigenous design in a contemporary world.

winners.name: Felicity Sundang

heritage.costume: Rungus festival dress in black tindal cloth with copper-coloured woven stripes, paired with full manik jewellery set and miniature gong instrument

winners.bio: Felicity is a community nurse in Johor who founded a KDM cultural club to keep young diaspora Rungus connected to their heritage. Her passion for community building earned her the MRK recognition at KDCA JB 2025.

winners.name: Kimberley Myra Terence

heritage.costume: Traditional Kadazan Dusun performance attire with ornamental beaded accessories and ceremonial regalia, performing at the KDCA Hall, Penampang

winners.bio: Kimberley Myra Terence, representing Beluran, was crowned State Sugandoi Kaamatan 2025 champion at the KDCA Hall, Penampang, in an event officiated by Datuk Seri Dr Jeffrey Kitingan. She described her Sugandoi Negeri 2025 victory as her second biggest title, having previously won Bintang Sandakanfm in 2023. Her soulful command of Kadazan-Dusun vocal tradition captivated both the audience and judges at the grand finale.

winners.name: Marcus Gununjuk

heritage.costume: Highland Dusun ceremonial dress in deep forest green, with handwoven sinamot wrap and traditional bamboo accessories

winners.bio: A highland farmer by profession and a singer of rare natural talent, Marcus's 2024 Sugandoi performance moved the Penampang audience with a poignant tontolian — a traditional Dusun epic poem sung to a haunting pentatonic melody, passed down through twelve generations of his family.

winners.name: Rosalinda Sintul

heritage.costume: Full Rungus ceremonial attire in black with intricate red geometric woven panels, brass ring ornaments, and a ceremonial gong sash

winners.bio: Rosalinda is one of the last practitioners of the moginakan — a Rungus chanted lament traditionally performed at healing ceremonies. Her 2023 Sugandoi performance introduced this vanishing vocal form to a new generation, earning a standing ovation and the judges' unanimous top score.
achievements: UNESCO Special Recognition for Endangered Vocal Arts

winners.name: Harison Apin

heritage.costume: Coastal Kadazan ceremonial attire in ocean blue and silver with distinctive suluk headdress and mother-of-pearl beaded vest

winners.bio: An IT professional in Kuala Lumpur who trains for Sugandoi every weekend, Harison's rich baritone voice has been compared to the deep resonance of the traditional gong. His 2025 KDCA KL win was powered by an original composition blending traditional pentatonic scales with contemporary KDM poetry.

winners.name: Cecilia Mintol

heritage.costume: Murut ceremonial dress in ochre and black sinamot weave, paired with layered copper bead necklaces and the traditional bamboo dance rattle

winners.bio: Cecilia's career in healthcare left little time for the arts — until the pandemic led her to rediscover her grandmother's songbook of traditional Murut lullabies. Her 2024 KDCA KL Sugandoi win was a deeply personal performance that brought the 40-year-old tunes back into the public consciousness.
achievements: Cultural Reconnection Story of the Year

winners.name: Bernard Kopin

heritage.costume: Kadazan Tuaran ceremonial wrap in indigo and gold, with the distinctive kagang cap and handmade bamboo flute used as a performance prop

winners.bio: A civil servant by day and a cultural activist by night, Bernard has spent years transcribing traditional Tuaran Kadazan melodies into sheet music so they can be taught in schools. His 2025 KDCA Putrajaya Sugandoi title validated years of quiet, dedicated work to keep these songs alive.

winners.name: Angelina Daut

heritage.costume: Dusun Keningau highland dress in crimson and ivory with handwoven banat shoulder cloth and a beaded songkop headdress passed down from her great-grandmother

winners.bio: Angelina works as a policy analyst in Putrajaya and brings the same rigour to her Sugandoi preparation. Her 2024 KDCA Putrajaya performance featured a rare three-part a cappella vocal technique used in traditional Dusun wedding ceremonies, performed entirely from memory.

winners.name: Julian Gansau

heritage.costume: Pinamorokan Papar ceremonial dress in red and black silk, featuring gold embroidered panels and the traditional suluk head tie worn during formal singing ceremonies

winners.bio: An engineer based in Shah Alam, Julian has been performing traditional KDM songs at community events since his university days. His 2025 KDCA Selangor Sugandoi win came after three consecutive runner-up finishes — his persistence is a reflection of the same spirit he applies to preserving his heritage.

winners.name: Theresa Midu

heritage.costume: Rungus ceremonial dress in deep black with vivid red and orange woven detail, adorned with rare glass bead ornaments and a ceremonial bamboo tray headdress

winners.bio: Theresa is the daughter of a Rungus bobohizan (high priestess) and learned to sing ritual chants before she could read. Now a teacher in Johor Bahru, she brings that sacred vocal knowledge to competitive Sugandoi with electrifying effect. Her 2025 KDCA JB performance left even the judges in respectful silence.

winners.name: Dyann Dharlla

heritage.costume: Traditional Sinuangga' ceremonial attire representing the Beluran district, adorned with hand-crafted manik beadwork and the ornate silver songkop headdress

winners.bio: Dyann Dharlla was crowned Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan Beluran 2026, taking home prizes worth thousands of ringgit — including RM5,000 cash and a travel package to Bali, Indonesia. Her grace, cultural depth, and commanding stage presence earned her the right to represent Beluran at the 2026 State Finals on 31 May at Hongkod Koisaan, Penampang, among 52 finalists from across Sabah and the diaspora.
achievements: Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan Beluran 2026 — District Champion
RM5,000 Cash Prize & Bali Travel Package
Beluran Representative — 66th Unduk Ngadau State Finals

winners.name: Finafayena Primus

heritage.costume: Lapoi — the traditional Dusun woven ceremonial wrap in rich indigo and gold, complemented by hand-crafted manik beadwork and the iconic siung headdress of dried seeds and brass

winners.bio: Finafayena Primus is the Juara Unduk Ngadau KDCA Klang Valley 2026 — a proud symbol of the KDM diaspora. Adorned in the Lapoi, the traditional Dusun ceremonial costume, she delivered a bilingual cultural address that moved the audience to a standing ovation, speaking of homeland, identity, and belonging from 1,600 kilometres away.
achievements: Juara Unduk Ngadau KDCA Klang Valley 2026
KDCA KL Young Voices Founding Member

winners.name: Wendey Merrylen S. Jasmine

heritage.costume: Sinuangga — Dusun interpretation in ivory and rose-gold with hand-woven tinangkong insert panels and a sculpted suyang headdress commissioned from a Ranau master artisan

winners.bio: Wendey Merrylen S. Jasmine is the Juara Unduk Ngadau KDCA Putrajaya 2026. A civil servant at the Ministry of Agriculture, she carries her Dusun roots into the corridors of Putrajaya, championing indigenous food heritage in national policy. Dressed in a Sinuangga Dusun interpretation, her victory was recognised for grace, cultural depth, and a powerful community voice.
achievements: Best Costume & Heritage Presentation — KDCA Putrajaya 2026

winners.name: Kristern Olwen Jafili

heritage.costume: Binadan — full Lotud traditional costume comprising the gomboton feathered headdress, kopo' black vest with colourful manik bead panels, layered warrior necklaces, and the ceremonial parang at the waist

winners.bio: Kristern Olwen Jafili, honoured with the title Buvazoi Tavantang — 'Guardian of the Cultural Flame' — is the 2026 MRK champion from KDCA Tuaran. Representing the Lotud people, he wore the Binadan ceremonial costume with pride. A secondary school history teacher, he has independently compiled a digital compendium of Lotud oral traditions, and was selected unanimously by the judges.
achievements: Buvazoi Tavantang Tuaran — KDCA Sabah MRK 2026

winners.name: Deanera Clarissa Jamdin

heritage.costume: Sinuangga — traditional Kadazan ceremonial dress in jet-black and gold, with hand-stitched manik beadwork, himpogot belt and songkop crown, representing the highland Kadazan identity carried across Borneo

winners.bio: Deanera Clarissa Jamdin is the Juara Unduk Ngadau KDCA Sarawak 2026, representing the Kadazan diaspora community in the Land of the Hornbill. Dressed in the Sinuangga, she brought the traditions of Sabah's highland Kadazan to the Sarawak stage with grace and cultural authority, earning a unanimous decision from the judging panel.
achievements: Best Cultural Knowledge Award — Sarawak Region

winners.name: Gelvia Vanessa Jenny

heritage.costume: Sia do Binokian — the signature Papar Kadazan ceremonial attire in rich maroon and silver, distinguished by its unique binokian (layered sash) woven from pandanus leaf strips, paired with intricate tinuang silver jewellery

winners.bio: Gelvia Vanessa Jenny, District Winner from KDCA Papar 2026, wore the distinctive Sia do Binokian — a costume unique to the Kadazan Papar community of coastal Sabah. Her confident presentation of the binokian weaving tradition and deep knowledge of coastal Kadazan harvest ceremonies set her apart as a champion of her district's heritage.
achievements: KDCA Papar District Winner — Unduk Ngadau 2026
Sia do Binokian Heritage Presentation Award

winners.name: TBA — Sugandoi Champion

heritage.costume: TBA

winners.bio: The 2026 Sugandoi champion will be announced at the Hari Kaamatan celebration. This space is reserved for the voice that will carry the song of the KDMR people into the new decade.

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Sinuangga — traditional Kadazan ceremonial blouse adorned with hand-beaded manik and gold filigree headdress, representing the KDM diaspora in southern Peninsular Malaysia

winners.bio: The KDCA Johor chapter is preparing to crown its first Unduk Ngadau champion. This space is reserved for the woman who will carry the torch of Kadazan-Dusun heritage across the Causeway, representing the KDM diaspora community in southern Peninsular Malaysia.
achievements: KDCA Johor Unduk Ngadau 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Sinuangga — Melaka-influenced interpretation with nutmeg-inspired beadwork motifs and the classic Rinogoo brass headdress

winners.bio: The historic city of Melaka will soon see its first KDCA Unduk Ngadau crowned. This placeholder honours the diaspora women who keep the Kadazan-Dusun flame alive far from the Borneo highlands, weaving heritage into every step they take.
achievements: KDCA Melaka Unduk Ngadau 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Sinuangga — island-inspired adaptation with pearl-inlaid beadwork reflecting Labuan's maritime identity and the iconic Simpay headdress

winners.bio: The Pearl of Borneo, Labuan, is preparing to crown its first KDCA Unduk Ngadau champion. This entry celebrates the island's growing KDMR diaspora and the cultural bridge between Labuan's free port spirit and the ancestral homeland.
achievements: KDCA WP Labuan Unduk Ngadau 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)
First KDCA WP Labuan chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Sinuangga — Penang-influenced interpretation with Peranakan beadwork accents and the ornate Simpay headdress, fusing KDM and northern Malaysian motifs

winners.bio: Pulau Pinang's vibrant cultural tapestry will soon welcome its first KDCA Unduk Ngadau champion. This placeholder honours the diaspora women who bridge the gap between island cosmopolitanism and the deep-rooted traditions of the Kadazan-Dusun highlands.
achievements: KDCA Pulau Pinang Unduk Ngadau 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)
First KDCA Pulau Pinang chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Simpak — warrior ceremonial vest with brass disc armour and the iconic Rungus Tinaukat headdress, symbolising diaspora strength

winners.bio: The KDCA Johor chapter is preparing to crown its first Momogun Rungus Kadazan champion. This space is reserved for the warrior who will carry the Momogun spirit across the Causeway, representing the KDM diaspora community in southern Peninsular Malaysia with pride and valour.
achievements: First KDCA Johor MRK chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Simpak — historic Melaka-influenced warrior vest with Portuguese-tile beadwork accents and the Tinaukat headdress

winners.bio: The historic city of Melaka will soon see its first KDCA MRK crowned. This placeholder honours the diaspora warriors who keep the Momogun flame alive far from the Borneo highlands, defending heritage in the streets where world cultures have met for centuries.
achievements: First KDCA Melaka MRK chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Simpak — island warrior vest with shell and pearl beadwork reflecting Labuan's maritime trading heritage and the Tinaukat headdress

winners.bio: The Pearl of Borneo, Labuan, is preparing to crown its first KDCA MRK champion. This entry celebrates the island's growing KDMR diaspora warrior tradition and the cultural bridge between Labuan's free port spirit and the ancestral Momogun homeland.
achievements: KDCA WP Labuan MRK 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)
First KDCA WP Labuan MRK chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Simpak — Penang-influenced warrior vest with Peranakan and colonial beadwork motifs and the Tinaukat headdress, fusing Momogun and northern Malaysian heritage

winners.bio: Pulau Pinang's vibrant cultural tapestry will soon welcome its first KDCA MRK champion. This placeholder honours the diaspora warriors who bridge the gap between island cosmopolitanism and the deep-rooted Momogun traditions of the Rungus highlands.
achievements: KDCA Pulau Pinang MRK 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)
First KDCA Pulau Pinang MRK chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Traditional Kadazan Dusun performance attire with embroidered Voni blouse and ornamental beaded accessories

winners.bio: The KDCA Johor chapter is preparing to crown its first Sugandoi singing champion. This space is reserved for the voice that will carry the songs of the KDMR diaspora across the Causeway, echoing the ancestral melodies of Sabah in the south of Peninsular Malaysia.
achievements: First KDCA Johor Sugandoi chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Traditional Kadazan Dusun performance attire with historic Melaka-influenced embroidery and ornamental beaded accessories

winners.bio: The historic city of Melaka will soon hear its first KDCA Sugandoi champion. This placeholder celebrates the diaspora singers who keep the Kadazan-Dusun vocal tradition alive, harmonising the old songs of Borneo with the multicultural rhythms of the Straits.
achievements: First KDCA Melaka Sugandoi chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Traditional Kadazan Dusun performance attire with island-inspired shell and pearl embroidery reflecting Labuan's maritime heritage

winners.bio: The Pearl of Borneo, Labuan, is preparing to crown its first KDCA Sugandoi champion. This entry celebrates the island's growing KDMR diaspora singing tradition and the cultural bridge between Labuan's free port spirit and the ancestral songs of the Kadazan-Dusun heartland.
achievements: KDCA WP Labuan Sugandoi 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)
First KDCA WP Labuan Sugandoi chapter representative

winners.name: TBA — Coming Soon

heritage.costume: Traditional Kadazan Dusun performance attire with Penang-influenced Peranakan embroidery motifs and ornamental beaded accessories

winners.bio: Pulau Pinang's vibrant cultural tapestry will soon welcome its first KDCA Sugandoi champion. This placeholder honours the diaspora singers who bridge the gap between island cosmopolitanism and the deep-rooted Kadazan-Dusun song traditions of the Borneo highlands.
achievements: KDCA Pulau Pinang Sugandoi 2026 — Champion (awaiting selection)
First KDCA Pulau Pinang Sugandoi chapter representative

winners.name: Ceelecia Reeka Mosungin

heritage.costume: Traditional Sinuangga' ceremonial attire adorned with hand-crafted manik beadwork and the ornate silver songkop headdress, representing the Sandakan district

winners.bio: Ceelecia Reeka Mosungin, 21, a student at Universiti Malaysia Sabah (Sandakan Campus), was crowned Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan Sandakan 2026 at the district-level celebration. Her youth and deep cultural knowledge made her a standout among the Sandakan finalists, earning her the honour of representing the district at the 2026 State Finals at Hongkod Koisaan on 31 May.
achievements: Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan Sandakan 2026 — District Champion
Sandakan Representative — 66th Unduk Ngadau State Finals

winners.name: Christebell Masius

heritage.costume: Highland Kadazan Dusun ceremonial attire in deep forest tones, featuring the distinctive suyang headdress and hand-pounded silver accessories of the Ranau tradition

winners.bio: Christebell Masius from Kampung Bongkud emerged as champion of the Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan Ranau 2026 final, held at Dewan Masyarakat Ranau. Her profound knowledge of highland Kadazan Dusun harvest traditions and her commanding stage presence secured her victory, earning the right to represent Ranau at the 66th Unduk Ngadau State Finals at Hongkod Koisaan on 31 May.
achievements: Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan Ranau 2026 — District Champion
Dewan Masyarakat Ranau Grand Final Winner
Ranau Representative — 66th Unduk Ngadau State Finals

winners.name: Liane Melve Grace Lias

heritage.costume: Contemporary-traditional Sinuangga' reflecting the cosmopolitan spirit of Kota Kinabalu, adorned with manik beadwork and the himpogot ceremonial sash

winners.bio: Liane Melve Grace Lias was officially named the Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan for Bandaraya Kota Kinabalu 2026, crowned at a celebratory event in the heart of Sabah's capital. Representing the urban face of KDM cultural pride, she carries the heritage of Kadazan Dusun tradition into the city stage and will advance to the 66th Unduk Ngadau State Finals at Hongkod Koisaan on 31 May.
achievements: Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan Bandaraya KK 2026 — District Champion
Bandaraya KK Representative — 66th Unduk Ngadau State Finals

songs.title: Pirousan do Bambazon
songs.quote: A voice that carries the sound of the Crocker Range — powerful, clear, and rooted in Kadazan tonal tradition. Performed entirely without instrumental backing.

songs.title: Rayuan Kinabalu
songs.quote: Sintul's interpretation of this classic Dusun ballad left the audience in silence — a rare feat at a competition stage.

songs.title: Momolou Oku
songs.quote: A devotional piece rooted in Ranau's highland tradition, performed with a clarity that resonated across generations in the audience.

songs.title: Tingkau Rungus
songs.quote: Drawing from the oral traditions of the Rungus people of Kota Belud, Harison brought a rarely-heard vocal style to the Sugandoi stage.

songs.title: Ginarang Dusun
songs.quote: An intimate, meditative performance of a harvest hymn from Nabawan — performed with technical mastery and deep cultural authenticity.

songs.title: Sumazau Bambazon
songs.quote: Kopin's baritone gave this ceremonial song from Tuaran a grounded, dignified quality that few have matched on the Sugandoi stage.

songs.title: Kadazan Rising
songs.quote: A contemporary arrangement blending traditional Kadazan melodies with modern vocal production — anchored in authentic indigenous heritage.

hallOfFame.name: Datuk Yee Moh Chai
hallOfFame.bio: A pioneering Kadazan political figure who championed indigenous rights and the formalization of the KDM identity in Sabah. Served multiple terms as a state assemblyman and was instrumental in cultural preservation efforts.

hallOfFame.name: Cleophas Mojuntin
hallOfFame.bio: Known as 'The Undying Eagle of Penampang,' Mojuntin was a founding father of Sabah politics and one of the most respected KDM leaders. He died tragically in the Rajah Brooke aircraft disaster in 1976.

hallOfFame.name: Judeth Justin Anad
hallOfFame.bio: A master of the sompoton and tongkungon, Judeth has dedicated her life to preserving traditional Kadazan Dusun musical heritage. She has performed at national and international folk festivals, bringing KDM music to global audiences.

hallOfFame.name: Arnold Panajou
hallOfFame.bio: One of Sabah's greatest long-distance runners, Arnold represented Malaysia at multiple SEA Games events. His achievements brought pride to the Murut community and paved the way for generations of KDM athletes.

hallOfFame.name: Dorina Ginsigau
hallOfFame.bio: A celebrated Rungus traditional dancer and costume designer who has worked to preserve authentic Rungus sumazau choreography and the intricate manik (beadwork) traditions of Northern Sabah.

hallOfFame.name: Dr. Bernard Godron Disimon
hallOfFame.bio: A linguistics professor at Universiti Malaysia Sabah, Dr. Disimon has published extensively on Kadazan-Dusun dialects and co-authored the first comprehensive Kadazan-Dusun dictionary for academic use.

hallOfFame.name: Richard Mojikon
hallOfFame.bio: A world-class silat practitioner who has won gold at multiple international championships. Richard is known for blending traditional Kadazan warrior philosophy with competitive silat, inspiring a new generation of KDM martial artists.

hallOfFame.name: Dayang Nurfaizah
hallOfFame.bio: One of Malaysia's most celebrated vocalists with Kadazan-Dusun roots, Dayang Nurfaizah has sold millions of albums nationally while proudly representing her heritage on the biggest stages in the country.

hallOfFame.name: Joniston Bangkuai
hallOfFame.bio: A committed servant of the Dusun people in the Mount Kinabalu highlands, Joniston has advocated tirelessly for eco-tourism rights, indigenous land protection, and rural community development in the Ranau district.
achievements: Mount Kinabalu Eco-Tourism Task Force chair

hallOfFame.name: Felicia Chin Juat Woan
hallOfFame.bio: Founder of Tambunan Tapai Co-op, Felicia has commercialized traditional rice wine (tapai) using sustainable fermentation methods, creating livelihoods for over 200 KDM families while preserving a centuries-old tradition.

hallOfFame.name: Sirinah Bondok
hallOfFame.bio: A master weaver of the traditional Rungus linangkit and tuhau cloth, Sirinah's intricate patterns have been exhibited at the National Museum of Malaysia and acquired by collectors across Southeast Asia.

hallOfFame.name: Vincent Pung
hallOfFame.bio: A stalwart midfielder for Sabah FA, Vincent captained the team during their historic return to the Malaysia Super League, becoming one of the most beloved footballers among the KDM community.

news.headline: RTK Randawi Tavantang Penampang 2026: Brendan Isaiah Tubong Crowned with RM29,000 Prize
news.summary: Brendan Isaiah Tubong was crowned winner of the Randawi Tavantang Kaamatan (RTK) Penampang 2026, taking home prizes worth RM29,000 including cash, a study scholarship, and sponsored goods and services. The RTK male pageant continues to grow, having already been showcased at KDCA Sarawak and set to represent Malaysia in a cultural exchange in Indonesia.

news.headline: State-Level Momohiis, Manangon & Momoroisol Competition Opens This Saturday at Hongkod Koisaan
news.summary: The public is invited to witness the 2026 state-level Momohiis, Manangon, and Momoroisol Competition at the Hongkod Koisaan Penampang Hall this Saturday, 16 May, in conjunction with Tadau Kaamatan. The competitions celebrate traditional KDM knowledge, weaving, and storytelling arts.

news.headline: Skyrocketing Airfares Crush Kaamatan Homecoming Hopes for Thousands of Sabahans
news.summary: Thousands of Sabahans working in Peninsular Malaysia may be forced to miss Kaamatan 2026 as airfares have surged beyond reach. A one-way KL–KK fare now commands between RM812 and RM1,347, driven by global fuel supply disruptions following the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

news.headline: CM Hajiji: Kaamatan 2026 Is a Festival That Unites All Sabahans Regardless of Background
news.summary: Chief Minister Datuk Seri Panglima Hajiji Noor urged every community in Sabah to participate in the month-long Kaamatan celebration, saying the harvest festival belongs to all Sabahans. He highlighted the 2026 theme 'Harvesting Unity, Celebrating Harmony' as a call for solidarity across all ethnic groups.

news.headline: 52 Finalists Confirmed for 66th Unduk Ngadau — Brand-New Two-Stage Grand Final Format
news.summary: KDCA has confirmed 52 finalists for the 66th Unduk Ngadau Kaamatan on 30–31 May at Hongkod Koisaan, Penampang. A new two-stage format sees pre-judging on May 30 narrow the field to 25, before a top 7 is named and one queen is crowned on May 31. Finalists represent districts across Sabah and KDCA branches in the Klang Valley, Putrajaya, Kuching, and Penang.

news.headline: Kaamatan 2026 Launched in Papar Under Theme 'Harvesting Unity, Celebrating Harmony'
news.summary: The 66th Kaamatan Harvest Festival was officially launched in Papar on 1 May 2026, marking the start of a month-long celebration across Sabah. The theme — 'Harvesting Unity, Celebrating Harmony' — reflects cultural preservation across generations and the shared spirit of Sabah's diverse indigenous communities.

liveEvent.title: Hari Kaamatan 2026 — State Final Live Arena
liveEvent.subtitle: Unduk Ngadau, MRK & Sugandoi · Hongkod Koisaan, Penampang · 30–31 May 2026
